sections.paragraph:
προφανερόω 1 aor. προεφανέρωσα, pass. προεφανερώθην reveal beforehand/in advance τὸ πάθος pass. B 6:7. τινὶ περί τινος reveal someth. to someone in advance 3:6. Without the dat., which is to be supplied fr. the context 11:1. τί τινι someth. to someone 7:1. Abs. (w. προετοιμάζειν and followed by πληροῦν, the ‘fulfilling’ of the revelation) MPol 14:2.—DELG s.v. φαίνω.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

someth. someth. = something

dat. dat. = dative

  1. fr. = from

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 889
